vulnerability.reference: Auditors' overreliance on SAE feature dictionaries without functional validation.
vulnerability.generated: Auditors have no ground-truth access to which SAE features are functionally important, relying solely on potentially spurious attributions.

impact.reference: False certification of safety leads to deployment of unsafe models.
impact.generated: Unsafe LLMs are falsely certified as safe, leading to deployment and real-world harm from overlooked model flaws.

dose_response.reference: SAEs recovering non-functional features enables auditors' overreliance on them for safety certification, which in scenario LLM safety auditing exposes false confidence, leading to deployment of unsafe models and eroded public trust.
dose_response.generated: SAE recovery of interpretable but functionally unimportant features, through safety auditors certifying LLM safety, triggers reliance on spurious attributions without ground-truth feature importance, leading to false safety certification and real-world harm from unsafe deployments.
